title: Bee Removal in Prospect Heights, Brooklyn | $350-$550 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in Prospect Heights, Brooklyn. Safe, humane bee extraction for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for $350-$550 service!
intro: Prospect Heights residents facing bee infestations need prompt, professional removal to protect their families and properties. Best Pest Control NYC offers expert bee removal services throughout Prospect Heights, from the historic brownstones near Grand Army Plaza to the modern condos along Flatbush Avenue. Our trained technicians safely remove bee colonies while prioritizing humane practices and environmental responsibility.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Prospect Heights for years, understanding the unique challenges of bee infestations in Brooklyn's diverse housing stock. Our licensed specialists use proven techniques to safely extract bees without harming your property, offering transparent pricing between $350-$550 and same-day emergency service when needed.
localContext: Prospect Heights' tree-lined streets, proximity to Prospect Park, and mix of historic brownstones and contemporary buildings create ideal conditions for bee colonies to establish nests in wall voids, chimneys, and eaves. The neighborhood's active community gardens and flowering trees near the Brooklyn Museum and Botanic Garden attract bees, making professional removal services essential when colonies move too close to homes.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove bees in Prospect Heights?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day bee removal services throughout Prospect Heights. Our team can typically arrive within 2-4 hours for emergency situations where bees pose an immediate threat to residents or visitors.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 bee removal cost include?
faqs.answer: Our bee removal service includes a thorough inspection, safe extraction of the entire colony, removal of honeycomb and hive materials, treatment of the affected area, and sealing of entry points to prevent future infestations. The final cost depends on hive location, size, and accessibility.

faqs.question: Do you relocate bees or exterminate them?
faqs.answer: Whenever possible, we prioritize humane bee relocation, working with local beekeepers to safely relocate colonies. For aggressive Africanized bees or situations where relocation isn't feasible, we use targeted methods that minimize environmental impact while ensuring your safety.

faqs.question: Can bees damage my Prospect Heights brownstone or apartment?
faqs.answer: Yes, established bee colonies can cause structural damage over time. Honey and wax can seep through walls, attract other pests, and create moisture problems. In brownstones and older buildings common in Prospect Heights, bees often nest in wall cavities, chimneys, or under eaves, making professional removal crucial to prevent costly repairs.
